parsed_review.summary: CP00-732 is a descriptor-only, runtime-closed Korean Legal Depth bridge pack (RP24.P03.M06.S13-RP24.P04.M03.S20, 150 units, risk C). Directly inspected via Read/Grep: registry.js (CP732 binding at L179 + requirements at L1682), service.js (descriptor/case-set/Hermes/Claude/handoff factories), validators.js (coverage + descriptor validators), index.js (re-exports all three), model.test.js (CP00-732 binding/coverage, runtime-closed, and contract-projection tests), contracts/korean-legal-depth-contract.json (current/latest_pack = CP00-732, latest_projection = KoreanLegalCp732P03P04ReviewUiBridgeDescriptor, validation.valid = true, CP722-731 preserved historical), manifest.json (production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status in_progress), and README CP00-732 entry. Deliverable distribution {test:13,hermes_evidence:7,claude_review:11,implementation:53,contract:17,security_audit:16,ui:30,fixture:3} and all 9 micro-title/micro-phase/phase counts match the prompt baseline and sum to 150; first/last unit IDs match the binding. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced by KOREAN_LEGAL_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION + ROW_EXTRAS + createCaseSet (descriptor_only true, runtime_execution false, no raw/legal/credential/client payloads) and actively asserted by validateDescriptor. Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-733; Hermes packets emit_runtime_receipt=false (deterministic control-plane review/receipt only, not product runtime). Review hardening satisfied: auth preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON, 1432-byte stdout, not 0-byte), forbidden_review_evidence list present (not_logged_in, malformed_json, usage_limit, tool_call_shaped_output, zero_byte_stdout, fenced_json_with_prose), tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob. Provenance: I relied on command-evidence.json for the full validation ladder + npm test + build + git diff --check (all exit_code 0) but did not re-execute them under this read-only review. The PENDING claude-review-result/construction-inspection/adjudication placeholders and absence of review-receipt.json/raw-output.json are the expected pre-review state this run finalizes; the manifest correctly does not yet assert production_ready or runtime_ready and makes no enterprise-trust claim. The descriptor-verified production_ready claim (flag korean_legal_p03_p04_review_ui_bridge_descriptor_verified) is fully supported. One P3 informational note about a benign dual-listing in the harness-generated changed-file-scope.json; no P0/P1/P2. Verdict PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; does not block pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: Pack evidence dir and handoff doc listed in both implementation and unrelated-preserved scope sets
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-732/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 175-176) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 182, 184) of the harness-generated changed-file-scope.json. This is a benign labeling overlap: the pack's own evidence directory is simultaneously an implementation artifact and a preserved dirty-worktree path. active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, so no required file was dropped, and the overlap has no effect on the descriptor-only deliverables, validators, contract projection, or the production_ready claim. It is a scope-manifest tooling artifact, not a CP00-732 deliverable defect.
findings.recommendation: Optional/non-blocking: have the changed-file-scope generator de-duplicate so a path classified as an untracked implementation file is not also emitted under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, to avoid reader confusion.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; does not block CP00-732 pack or goal closeout. No action required for this pack.
